title: Eyes on arts: Upcoming entertainment highlights
bodyText:
GEORGE ENTERTAINMENT NEWS - Georgians can pick and choose their weekend entertainment from a large variety of events.Below is our weekly calendar for the arts: Eyes on arts. These entries are sent to George Herald on a weekly basis.

Have a look and book your tickets for some stunning entertainment on your doorstep.


> 15 March


Presentation on the making of Obie Oberholzer's new book Going Dutch (12:00) at George Arts Theatre.

Going Dutch is Oberholzer's 16th publication, and covers nine South African towns with Dutch names and their mother cities in the Netherlands.

Admission: R100. Contact Paul Rixom on 072 525 0016.

A typical exemplar of Obie Oberholzer's people photography: the owner of Bergalleen Farm, Bennie van der Linda, holds his Australian cattle dog, Max, in a lapa on this farm near the small town of Marken in Northen Limpopo. There is also a Marken in Holland, that's why this photo features in Oberholzer's book, Going Dutch.


> 15 Maart


Hoofseun met Tollie Parton (19:30) by George-kunsteteater. Hoofseun is 'n komiese kykie na wat dit werklik beteken om 'n klein Afrikaanse dorpie se verwagtinge in die gesig te staar, as 'n fopdosser. Koop kaartjies by www.wynvirdiepyn.com teen R200 per persoon. Streng ouderdomsbeperking - geen kinders onder 18 jaar.


> 15 March


Magical Moments of Song (19:00) at the Dias Museum in Mossel Bay. Much-loved soprano Sandra Nel and tenor Chris Coetzer perform in a benefit concert in aid of Santos Haven Frail Care in Mossel Bay. Tickets cost R150 and can be booked with Abé Meintjes of ArtSpace (083 268 3718).


> 24 March


Auditions for children (8 - 17) for The Sound of Music (18:00) at George Arts Theatre. Register online at www.georgeartstheatre.co.za. More information will follow after registration.


> 26 March


Auditions for adults (18 plus) for The Sound of Music (18:00) at George Arts Theatre. Register online at www.georgeartstheatre.co.za. More information will follow after registration.


> 29 March


Fragments of Sound (15:00 & 19:00) at the Market Community Theatre. A vibrant dance show from the new studio, Bradwell Dance Collective. Local dance teacher Bradley Zeelie takes his company to greater heights. Tickets cost R100. For information contact 081 070 9661.


> 5 April


Steve Newman, Auriol Hays and Dan Boshoff: Songs from around the way (19:00) at the Market Community Theatre. South African musician Steve Newman is regarded as a maestro and is critically acclaimed for his original compositions and virtuosic playing. Auriol Hays originally hails from the Garden Route, as does Dan Boshoff.

Hayes is renowned for her passionate voice and storytelling, and guitarist Dan Boshoff has been playing, recording and touring with a range of original projects over the last 20 years. Tickets cost R160 per person.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
